pysnap.core package¶

Core business logic package for PySnap.

Submodules¶

pysnap.core.appliance module¶

Helpers for reading appliance metadata from OVF and OVA files.

pysnap.core.appliance.read_appliance_vm_names(image_path)[source]¶

Read original VM names declared inside an OVF or OVA appliance.

Parameters:

image_path (str | Path) – Appliance path.

Returns:

Declared appliance VM names in descriptor order.

Raises:

PySnapError – If the descriptor cannot be read or parsed.

Return type:

tuple[str, …]

pysnap.core.models module¶

Domain models used by PySnap.

class pysnap.core.models.VMReference(name, uuid)[source]¶

Bases: object

Represent a lightweight VirtualBox VM reference.

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • uuid (str)

name: str¶
uuid: str¶
class pysnap.core.models.SerialPortConfiguration(enabled, mode=None, port=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Represent the raw UART1 configuration of one VM.

Parameters:

  • enabled (bool)

  • mode (str | None)

  • port (int | None)

enabled: bool¶
mode: str | None = None¶
port: int | None = None¶
class pysnap.core.models.ImportCandidate(vsys_index, vm_name, group, requires_eula_accept=False)[source]¶

Bases: object

Represent a VM discovered in an appliance import dry run.

Parameters:

  • vsys_index (int)

  • vm_name (str)

  • group (str)

  • requires_eula_accept (bool)

vsys_index: int¶
vm_name: str¶
group: str¶
requires_eula_accept: bool = False¶
class pysnap.core.models.VMInfo(name, uuid, groups, serial_port=None, vm_state=None, parent_name=None, managed=False, metadata=<factory>)[source]¶

Bases: object

Represent the VM information required by the CLI.

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • uuid (str)

  • groups (tuple[str, ...])

  • serial_port (int | None)

  • vm_state (str | None)

  • parent_name (str | None)

  • managed (bool)

  • metadata (dict[str, str])

name: str¶
uuid: str¶
groups: tuple[str, ...]¶
serial_port: int | None = None¶
vm_state: str | None = None¶
parent_name: str | None = None¶
managed: bool = False¶
metadata: dict[str, str]¶
property primary_group: str¶

Return the primary VM group.

Returns:

Primary group name or /Others when unavailable.

class pysnap.core.models.VMGroup(name, vm_names)[source]¶

Bases: object

Represent a VM group and its members.

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • vm_names (tuple[str, ...])

name: str¶
vm_names: tuple[str, ...]¶
class pysnap.core.models.IntegrationTestResult(machines, deleted_vm_names, monitor_records=())[source]¶

Bases: object

Represent the outcome of an integration test run.

Parameters:

  • machines (tuple[VMInfo, ...])

  • deleted_vm_names (tuple[str, ...])

  • monitor_records (tuple[VMMonitorRecord, ...])

machines: tuple[VMInfo, ...]¶
deleted_vm_names: tuple[str, ...]¶
monitor_records: tuple[VMMonitorRecord, ...] = ()¶
class pysnap.core.models.VMMonitorRecord(name, display_state, serial_port, group, raw_state)[source]¶

Bases: object

Represent a compact monitor record for a VM.

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • display_state (str)

  • serial_port (int | None)

  • group (str)

  • raw_state (str)

display_state: str¶

group: str¶
raw_state: str¶

pysnap.core.service module¶

Application service layer for PySnap.

pysnap.core.service.normalize_group_name(group)[source]¶

Normalize a VirtualBox group name.

Parameters:

group (str | None) – Raw group name.

Returns:

Group name that always starts with /.

Return type:

str

class pysnap.core.service.PySnapService(client=None, session_registry=None, serial_probe_factory=None, proto_settings_store=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Provide the main PySnap business operations.

Parameters:

  • client (VBoxManageClient | None)

  • session_registry (SessionRegistry | None)

  • serial_probe_factory (SerialProbeFactory | None)

  • proto_settings_store (ProtoSettingsStore | None)

SNAPSHOT_NAME = 'pysnap-base'¶
DEFAULT_SERIAL_TCP_PORT = 1024¶
DEFAULT_START_TIMEOUT = 30.0¶
DEFAULT_STOP_TIMEOUT = 60.0¶
DEFAULT_INTEGRATION_STOP_TIMEOUT = 180.0¶
STOPPED_STATES = {'aborted', 'poweroff', 'saved'}¶
PAUSED_STATES = {'paused'}¶
ERROR_STATES = {'gurumeditation', 'stuck'}¶
import_image(image_path, vm_name=None, progress_callback=None)[source]¶

Import an OVA or OVF image into VirtualBox.

Parameters:

  • image_path (str) – Path to the OVA or OVF appliance.

  • vm_name (str | None) – Optional custom name for a single imported VM.

  • progress_callback (Callable[[int], None] | None) – Optional import progress callback.

Returns:

Imported VM information objects.

Raises:

PySnapError – If the file is invalid or import does not create VMs.

Return type:

list[VMInfo]

run_integration_test(image_path, name_token=None)[source]¶

Run an end-to-end integration scenario on an appliance.

The scenario imports one appliance VM, creates three linked clones connected in a triangle of internal networks, captures machine details, validates runtime monitoring by attaching to one clone and starting a second clone headlessly, and removes all created VMs one by one.

Parameters:

  • image_path (str) – Path to the appliance used for the test.

  • name_token (str | None) – Optional deterministic suffix for generated VM names.

Returns:

Integration test result with machine info and deletion order.

Raises:

PySnapError – If the appliance is invalid or the test fails.

Return type:

IntegrationTestResult

list_groups()[source]¶

List all VM groups and their members.

Returns:

Group information sorted by group name.

Return type:

list[VMGroup]

list_monitored_vms()[source]¶

List VMs that are running or changing state in a compact form.

Returns:

Monitor records sorted by VM name.

Return type:

list[VMMonitorRecord]

get_monitor_state_label(vm_name)[source]¶

Return the user-facing runtime label for one VM.

Parameters:

vm_name (str) – VM name.

Returns:

User-facing monitor label.

Return type:

str

prepare_vm_connection(vm_name, timeout=None)[source]¶

Ensure that a VM is ready for an interactive serial connection.

Parameters:

  • vm_name (str) – VM name.

  • timeout (float | None) – Optional startup timeout in seconds.

Returns:

Updated VM information.

Raises:

PySnapError – If the VM cannot be connected.

Return type:

VMInfo

stop_runtime_vm(vm_name, timeout=None)[source]¶

Request a graceful stop for one running VM.

Parameters:

  • vm_name (str) – VM name.

  • timeout (float | None) – Optional timeout in seconds.

Raises:

PySnapError – If the VM is not in a stoppable state.

Return type:

None

stop_all_runtime_vms(timeout=None)[source]¶

Stop all currently running VMs that are in a normal running state.

Parameters:

timeout (float | None) – Optional per-VM timeout in seconds.

Returns:

Names of VMs that received a stop request and stopped.

Return type:

list[str]

show_vm(vm_name)[source]¶

Return details for a single VM.

Parameters:

vm_name (str) – Name of the VM to inspect.

Returns:

Detailed VM information.

Raises:

VMNotFoundError – If the VM is absent.

Return type:

VMInfo

plug_vm(vm_name)[source]¶

Configure one VM for PySnap serial-console connections.

If UART1 is already configured as a TCP server, the VM is returned unchanged. Otherwise, PySnap assigns a free TCP port and enables the same UART1 tcpserver mode used by the imported protocol images.

Parameters:

vm_name (str) – VM name to configure.

Returns:

Updated VM information.

Raises:

PySnapError – If the VM cannot be configured safely.

Return type:

VMInfo

clone_vm(base_vm, clone_vm, serial_port=None, networks=())[source]¶

Create a linked clone and configure it.

Parameters:

  • base_vm (str) – Name of the source VM.

  • clone_vm (str) – Name of the linked clone to create.

  • serial_port (int | None) – Optional TCP port assigned to UART1.

  • networks (tuple[str, ...]) – Up to three internal network names.

Returns:

Detailed information about the created clone.

Raises:

PySnapError – If invalid parameters are supplied.

Return type:

VMInfo

register_proto_settings_vm(vm_name)[source]¶

Persist one base VM name in the proto-settings store.

Parameters:

vm_name (str) – Base VM name to register.

Returns:

Updated configured VM names.

Raises:

VMNotFoundError – If the VM does not exist.

Return type:

tuple[str, …]

erase_vm(vm_name)[source]¶

Erase one VM when no dependent clones exist.

Parameters:

vm_name (str) – Name of the VM to remove.

Raises:

VMDependencyError – If dependent clones still exist.

Return type:

None

erase_group(group_name)[source]¶

Erase all VMs from a single group.

Parameters:

group_name (str) – Group to erase.

Returns:

Names of removed VMs.

Raises:

PySnapError – If the group is empty or contains blocked base VMs.

Return type:

list[str]

erase_all()[source]¶

Erase all registered VirtualBox VMs.

Returns:

Names of removed VMs.

Return type:

list[str]

PySnap
